accepted.reason: Compressible Euler flux and Jacobian-eigenvalue calculations are high-value scientific kernels for optimization.
PRNGs plus Halton/Van-der-Corput generation provide diverse loop-heavy numeric patterns with clear acceleration potential.
Dynamic time warping DP matrix updates are classic performance-sensitive sequence kernels.
Compact biophysical conductance formula is a useful numeric function for regression and stability testing.
Monte Carlo Pi estimation with parallel workers is a practical stochastic-compute benchmark.
CIGAR soft-clip parsing and threshold logic are portable branch-heavy data-processing routines.

skipped.reason: Contains obvious typos and minimal toy logic; low quality as a reusable GRPO example.
Broken/incomplete variable references and environment-specific file I/O reduce reliability and portability.
Tightly coupled to project-specific game state and Torch model APIs, making standalone use impractical.
Test harness code for domain-specific simulation internals rather than an independent optimization kernel.
